name: Tex-Mex Pork Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the pork shoulder of excess fat and cut into 1-1/2 inch chunks.
2. Bring all ingredients to a boil in a 4-quart pot, cover tightly, reduce heat, and simmer for 2-1/2 hours until meat is tender.
3. Alternatively, place all ingredients in a crockpot and cook on LOW for the entire day.
4. When ready to serve, discard any fat that has risen to the top.
5. This stew can be made in advance and stored refrigerated or frozen for up to 5 days or 3 months respectively.
6. To assemble: combine basic stew ingredients (pork, stock, onions, garlic) with vegetables, herbs, and tomato sauce in a 3-quart pot. Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
7. Whisk the tomato sauce with flour until blended, then stir into the stew and simmer uncovered for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until slightly thickened.
